preview_text:
The model backs Senegal to win at 61%, with Iraq holding a 12% chance of an upset. The sportsbook consensus is substantially more confident in a Senegal victory, assigning it 76%, leaving Iraq just 8% to take three points.

The model sees this as a moderately competitive match, with the draw probability at 27%—suggesting a genuine path to a stalemate outcome. That 27-point gap between a Senegal win and an Iraqi win indicates the favorite is favored, but not overwhelmingly so in the model's view. The draw's relative strength signals realistic parity in execution and chance outcomes.

The market and Polymarket are notably more bullish on Senegal than the model, with both assigning the favorites a win probability around 77% while compressing the draw to 15%. This represents a gap of roughly 15 percentage points on the Senegal win line. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is more accurate; users should note the divergence and consider what information sportsbooks and prediction markets may be pricing differently than the statistical model.
